lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. COLLEGE CAMPUS – MORNING. Students move between buildings.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ZARA waves back. Smiles. Laughs. Keeps walking.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Her phone vibrates constantly. Messages. Mentions. Notifications.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Look at her. Everyone knows her.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 1 – VOLLEYBALL PRACTICE. Zara leads drills. Teammates trust her.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ZARA: Don't worry. I've done worse.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: She's basically the center of the group.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Her smile disappears for a split second when nobody is looking. Then returns.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: Hiding emotions behind a social mask.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 2 – CAMPUS CAFÉ. Zara sits with several friends. Conversation never stops.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Everybody talks to Zara. Nobody asks Zara anything meaningful.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: Surrounded by people, lacking deeper connection.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 3 – SOCIAL MEDIA. Zara posts a volleyball team photo.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Within minutes: 150 likes. Comments flood in.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Hundreds of messages in her inbox. Most are: 'Hey', 'Can you send notes?', 'Coming tonight?', 'Need your help'.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: She talks to a lot of people.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: How many could she call at 2 AM?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: Many connections, few emotional bonds.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 4 – THE PHONE CALL. Evening. Her phone rings. MOM.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: MOTHER: Your father signed the papers.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: MOTHER: I thought you should know.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because she's been living with it for years.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Fragments appear. Arguments behind closed doors. Cancelled family trips. Long silences at dinner. Pretending everything was normal.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 5 – THE LOCKER. After practice. Zara opens her locker.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: A photo slips out. A smiling boy stands beside her.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: He was the one person she trusted.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Memories appear. Late-night calls. Sharing fears. Talking about her parents. Talking about her future. Talking about everything.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So she lost more than a relationship.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: She lost the one place she felt understood.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: Betrayal after emotional vulnerability.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 6 – PARTY NIGHT. Student apartment. Music. Laughter. Crowded room.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Everyone wants a photo. Everyone wants a conversation. Everyone seems happy she's there.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: She's talked to half the room.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Hours pass. Conversations come and go. Near midnight.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Zara quietly steps onto the balcony. Nobody notices.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: Feeling invisible despite popularity.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 7 – THE BREAKING POINT. Late night. Zara sits on her bed. Phone in hand.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: She scrolls through contacts. Stops. Keeps scrolling. Stops again.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Which one knows what's really wrong?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Zara begins crying. Not because she's alone. Because she doesn't know who she can trust anymore.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: CLUE ADDED: No trusted emotional support.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: SCENE 8 – THE QUESTION. Next day. Campus lawn.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: An old teammate sits beside her. No jokes. No small talk.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: TEAMMATE: No. Are you actually okay?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Long silence. The longest in the chapter.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: For the first time. Someone stays. No advice. No solutions. Just listening.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Why does that feel so important?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because somebody finally asked about her. Not the captain. Not the popular girl. Her.

scenes.title: Who Really Knows Zara?
scenes.instructions: Sort each interaction into Social Interaction or Meaningful Connection.

signals.explanation: Likes are a measure of visibility, not intimacy.

signals.statement: Someone noticed she seemed quieter than usual.
signals.explanation: Noticing a shift in behavior requires genuine attention to her emotional state.
Being included in a group activity doesn't mean she felt seen as an individual.

signals.statement: A friend asks how she's coping with her parents' separation.
signals.explanation: Addressing a specific, painful reality shows a willingness to carry the emotional weight.
Routine social obligations don't equate to emotional support.

signals.statement: Someone remembers something she shared months ago.
signals.explanation: Retention of small details proves someone was truly listening.
Casual pleasantries reinforce her popularity, but not her emotional safety.

signals.statement: Someone stays and listens without trying to fix the problem.
signals.explanation: Holding space for someone's pain is the highest form of connection.

scenes.title: The Mask
scenes.instructions: Sort each statement into What Zara Shows or What Zara Feels.

signals.explanation: A leadership role that projects strength and capability.
A physical reflex used to put others at ease.
The external perception of her social standing.
The persona she adopts to meet expectations.
Busy schedules often mask internal emptiness.
Being there for others while nobody is there for her.
The hidden trauma from a past betrayal.
The silent grief she carries alone.
The consequence of relying entirely on a social mask.
The core paradox of her existence.

signals.statement: Misses the person she once confided in
signals.explanation: The longing for the deep connection she lost.

signals.statement: Exhausted from pretending everything is okay
signals.explanation: The toll of maintaining the perfect image.

scenes.title: Chapter Complete
scenes.summary: Loneliness doesn't always come from having nobody around. Sometimes it comes from carrying your real self so deep inside that nobody ever gets the chance to know it. Zara learned that being surrounded by people isn't the same as being understood, and that letting the mask fall is the only way to find genuine connection.

scoreUpdate.awareness: based on identifying masks and true connections
